text: The patient is positioned in the right lateral decubitus position in preparation for the robotic left adrenalectomy procedure.
A 12mm camera trocar is inserted in the left midclavicular line, approximately 10 centimeters above the umbilicus, providing a visual entry point for the robotic adrenalectomy procedure.
The surgeon places an eight millimeter left arm trocar approximately seven centimeters superior to the previously positioned camera trocar in the left midclavicular line.

The surgeon places the right arm trocar approximately seven centimeters inferior and slightly lateral to the previously positioned camera trocar, establishing access for the robotic left adrenalectomy.

The surgeon positions the fourth robotic arm approximately five centimeters inferior and four centimeters lateral to the previously placed right robotic arm, completing the setup for the robotic left adrenalectomy.
The surgeon places a twelve millimeter assistant port in the midline, positioned cephalad to the umbilicus.
The surgeon begins the adrenalectomy procedure by reflecting the descending colon medially, exposing the underlying structures for further dissection.
The surgeon incises the peritoneum lateral to the spleen in a cephalad direction, facilitating the superior medial reflection of the spleen during the dissection.
The surgeon uses the fourth robotic arm to frequently retract tissues, optimizing the surgical field visibility during the adrenalectomy.
The fourth robotic arm applies gentle superior medial pressure on the spleen, facilitating the development of the plane between the superior medial aspect of the kidney and the spleen.
The robotic fourth arm applies anterior pressure on the lower pole of the left kidney, facilitating the visual identification of the left gonadal vein.
The surgeon continues the dissection in a cephalad direction towards the left renal vein, following the identification of anatomical structures.
The surgeon divides the tissue medial to the left renal vein, enhancing the exposure of the vein for further dissection.
Following the dissection of the left renal vein, the surgeon identifies the left adrenal vein located on the cephalad aspect of the left renal vein.
The surgeon ligates the left adrenal vein using two Hem-o-lok clips and then divides the vein, completing the separation from the left renal vein.
Following the division of the adrenal vein, the surgeon divides the tissue located inferior and medial to the adrenal gland, further exposing the surgical field.
The surgeon uses the fourth arm of the robotic system to retract the adrenal gland, facilitating its final extirpation from the surrounding tissues.
The surgeon places the adrenalectomy specimen into a retrieval bag and carefully removes it from the surgical field.
